examples.title: E-Waste Collection Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for optimizing e-waste collection routes
examples.code:
PROCEDURE OptimizeCollectionRoute(CollectionCenters, VehicleCapacity)
BEGIN
 Set RouteList = []
 Set RemainingCapacity = VehicleCapacity
 Set CurrentLocation = Depot
 
 FOR EACH center IN CollectionCenters DO
 IF center.DistanceFrom(CurrentLocation) < Threshold THEN
 IF RemainingCapacity >= center.EWasteQuantity THEN
 Add center TO CurrentRoute
 RemainingCapacity = RemainingCapacity - center.EWasteQuantity
 ELSE
 Add CurrentRoute TO RouteList
 CurrentRoute = []
 RemainingCapacity = VehicleCapacity
 ENDIF
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 RETURN RouteList
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm optimizes collection routes for e-waste vehicles to maximize efficiency while respecting vehicle capacity constraints.

examples.title: E-Waste Segregation Process
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SegregateEWaste(Item)
BEGIN
 IF Item.ContainsHazardousComponents() THEN
 Classify as HazardousWaste
 Store in SecureContainer
 ELSE
 IF Item.IsRepairable() THEN
 Classify for Reuse
 Send to RefurbishmentUnit
 ELSE
 Classify for Recycling
 IF Item.HasMagneticMaterials() THEN
 SeparateMagneticMaterials()
 ENDIF
 IF Item.HasMetals() THEN
 SeparateUsingEddyCurrent()
 ENDIF
 SeparatePlastics()
 SeparateGlass()
 ENDIF
 ENDIF
 RETURN Classification
END
examples.explanation: This procedure handles initial segregation of e-waste items based on their material composition and condition.

examples.title: EPR Compliance Check
examples.description: Algorithm to verify producer compliance with EPR obligations
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CheckEPRCompliance(Producer, ReportingPeriod)
BEGIN
 Set TargetCollection = Producer.GetCollectionTarget(ReportingPeriod)
 Set ActualCollected = Producer.GetTotalCollected()
 Set TargetRecycling = Producer.GetRecyclingTarget(ReportingPeriod)
 Set ActualRecycled = Producer.GetTotalRecycled()
 
 Set CollectionAchieved = (ActualCollected / TargetCollection) * 100
 Set RecyclingAchieved = (ActualRecycled / TargetRecycling) * 100
 
 IF CollectionAchieved >= 100 AND RecyclingAchieved >= 100 THEN
 RETURN "Compliant"
 ELSE IF CollectionAchieved >= 70 THEN
 RETURN "Partially Compliant - Warning"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Non-Compliant - Penalty Required"
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm checks if producers meet their Extended Producer Responsibility targets for collection and recycling.
